name: Process Masquerading (Windows)
notes:
This rule triggers when a sensitive process is executing from a non-legitimate directory. An attacker can masquerade a legitimate process to avoid detection, thereby execute malicious commands from the non-legitimate process.

Note: The Reference Set Sensitive Process Names is populated with Known Sensitive Processes. Tune this reference set with Processes to watch.
The reference map set Default Process Name and Process Directories has been pre-populated with sensitive process names and directories. Tune this reference data with the default location of the Sensitive Processes.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Path (custom) is not N/A, Process Name (custom) is not N/A
when any of Process Name (custom) are contained in any of Sensitive Process Names - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when any of Process Name (custom) is the key and any of Process Path (custom) is the value in any of Default Process Name and Process Directories - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY Process Masquerading (Windows) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Path (custom) is not N/A, Process Name (custom) is not N/A
AND when any of Process Name (custom) are contained in any of Sensitive Process Names - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND NOT when any of Process Name (custom) is the key and any of Process Path (custom) is the value in any of Default Process Name and Process Directories - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event description: Detects when a non-legitimate process is masqueraded as a legitimate process.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
